programmes.show_description: Joining Radio 5 Live.
Mark Stephen and Rachel Stewart with stories from the great outdoors.
News from across Scotland and the world, plus sport, weather, entertainment and big name interviews.
Maisie Adam, Caroline Weir and Mark Nelson chat to Amy Irons and Steven Mill as they kick-off the weekend with lots of laughs, great guests and timeless tunes.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
Open All Mics with all the goal action and big moments from across Scottish football, including the option of Hearts v Motherwell commentary. (KO 15:00).
Full commentary live from Tynecastle as Premiership leaders Hearts take on Motherwell (KO 15:00).
Open All Mics with all the goal action and big moments from across Scottish football, including the option of Hearts v Motherwell commentary. (KO 15:00).
Michael Stewart and Stephen Craigan are joined by the fans debating the big talking points following Saturday's action.
For the very best in traditional music, join Gary Innes for Take the Floor. We share with you once again the fantastic debut session from Rory Matheson and his Scottish Dance Band.
Mìcheal Steele with a selection of the best in pipe music. This week, he features sets recorded at the 2026 Donald MacLeod Memorial Competition.
This year's BBC Introducing Scottish Act of the Year winner Alice Faye goes On the Record. Snail Mail are on Heavy Rotation, plus tracks from Happy Mondays and Trashcan Sinatras.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
The best bits from the past seven days of the BBC's Scottish news podcast, with Martin Geissler.
An act of worship by The Very Reverend John Conway, Provost of St Mary's Episcopal Cathedral Edinburgh.
Tony's This Spiritual Life guest is An American Marriage author: Tayari Jones.
News, discussion and interviews from politics and beyond.
Falkirk host Rangers in their final Scottish Premiership match before the split, and all the action is live on Sportsound.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
Join Gary as he shares your special messages and dedications and plays your favourite Scottish Music requests.
Music by Korngold and De Falla sits alongside Mendelssohn's rarely heard concerto for two pianos, and John Butt plays Bach.
Settle in as Roddy Hart plays handpicked tracks, breakthrough artists, and fresh sounds to close out the weekend.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
